title: Goyazia villosa
taxonomy: Goyazia villosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. The genus Goyazia is part of the subfamily Faboideae. It is native to Brazil and is a member of the pea family, which is one of the largest and most diverse groups of flowering plants. The classification of Goyazia villosa also includes its order, which is Fabales. This classification system helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the plant.

intro: Goyazia villosa is a medicinal plant species native to Brazil, belonging to the family Apiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Goyazia villosa include essential oils, flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Goyazia villosa is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
